title: Gratitude Receipt v0.1
description: A JSON Schema for recording non-monetary value recognition in the Kazene Model of Network Intelligence (KMNI). A gratitude receipt links an issuer to a target trace and records how that trace influenced an actor, agent, document, protocol, or system.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this gratitude receipt.

gratitude_id.description: Unique identifier for the gratitude receipt.

issuer_id.description: Identifier of the human, AI agent, model, repository, organization, protocol, or workflow issuing the gratitude receipt.

issuer_type.description: Type of entity issuing the gratitude receipt.

display_name.description: Optional display name of the issuer.

role.description: Optional role of the issuer in the workflow, such as reviewer, user, generator, verifier, editor, maintainer, or protocol_actor.

model_family.description: Optional model family or agent family when issuer_type is ai_agent or model.

target_trace_id.description: The trace ID receiving gratitude. This should refer to a trace registered by Existence Proof OS.

source_output_id.description: Optional identifier for the output, document, task, protocol, or system that was influenced by the target trace.

source_output_reference.description: Optional reference to the influenced output, such as a file path, repository path, URI, or internal pointer.

contribution_type.description: Type of contribution recognized by this gratitude receipt.

impact_score.description: Contextual estimate of how strongly the target trace influenced the issuer or source output. 0 means no meaningful impact; 1 means extremely high impact.

confidence.description: Optional confidence level for the gratitude claim. 0 means very uncertain; 1 means highly confident.

context.description: Human-readable explanation of why gratitude was issued and how the trace contributed.

evidence.description: Optional evidence references supporting the gratitude claim.

evidence_type.description: Type of evidence used to support the gratitude claim.

reference.description: Reference value, such as trace ID, file path, commit hash, URI, citation, or internal pointer.

note.description: Optional explanation of the evidence reference.

issued_at.description: Timestamp when the gratitude receipt was issued.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for operational use. Historical records may remain archived even after expiration.

visibility.description: Visibility level of this gratitude receipt.

verification.description: Optional verification metadata for the gratitude receipt.

verification_method.description: Method used to verify this gratitude receipt.

signature.description: Optional cryptographic signature or attestation value.

public_key_reference.description: Optional reference to the public key used for signature verification.

verified.description: Whether the gratitude receipt has passed the stated verification method.

verified_at.description: Optional timestamp when verification was completed.

anti_abuse.description: Optional anti-abuse metadata used to reduce gratitude spam, collusion, and artificial trust inflation.

duplicate_check.description: Whether duplicate gratitude detection was performed.

rate_limit_checked.description: Whether issuer rate limits were checked.

conflict_of_interest_flag.description: Whether a potential conflict of interest was detected or declared.

abuse_risk_score.description: Estimated abuse risk score. 0 means low risk; 1 means high risk.

review_required.description: Whether manual or governance review is required before this receipt is used for trust or allocation.

tags.description: Optional tags for classification and search.

status.description: Current status of the gratitude receipt.

status_note.description: Optional explanation of the current gratitude receipt status.

examples.context: The target trace provided the conceptual basis for defining Gratitude OS as the non-monetary value recognition layer of KMNI.
